title: Fractions, Decimals, Ratios, and Proportional Structure · Mastery Check
summary: Scale recipes, medication-free training examples, mixtures, maps, and staffing plans without changing relationships. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a proportional scaling and comparison workbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent fractions as part-whole, quotient, ratio, operator, and location meanings in context.
Add, subtract, multiply, and divide fractions by reasoning about units and equivalent forms.
Convert among fractions, decimals, and terminating or repeating representations without losing meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 22 units, changes by −9 units, then by +8 units. Calculate the final quantity. Use the result to decide Proportion Test for a proportional scaling and comparison workbook.
steps.why: The Proportion Test result for a proportional scaling and comparison workbook is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test proportionality with tables, graphs, equations, and constant ratios.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Before calculating Proportion Test, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for a proportional scaling and comparison workbook. hints: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale.
